summary: Tested iteration 61 features: Smart expense allocation to current accounts with 3 modes (topup_existing, create_new, allow_negative). All backend API tests pass (11/11). Frontend UI tests verified: dropdown visible on validated AND completed expenses for Admin, window.prompt triggered for insufficient balance, correct toast notifications, account top-up with correct label format. Manager/Gérante correctly does NOT see the dropdown (admin-only feature).
critical_code_review_comments: Backend expenses.py lines 692-815: SmartAllocateBody model and POST /expenses/{id}/allocate-account-smart endpoint correctly implements 3 modes with proper validation
Backend expenses.py lines 761-786: topup_existing mode correctly calculates missing amount, increments total_advance, and adds top_ups[] entry with label 'Recharge auto pour <description>'
Backend expenses.py lines 728-754: create_new mode creates new account with total_advance = expense.amount and correct naming
Backend expenses.py lines 787-788: allow_negative mode performs allocation without top-up, allowing negative balance
Frontend CaissePage.jsx lines 1015-1076: allocateExpenseToAccount() correctly triggers window.prompt when balance < amount with 3 options
Frontend AchatsTab.jsx lines 1662-1684: Funding source dropdown for approved expenses with data-testid='funding-source-{expense.id}'
Frontend AchatsTab.jsx lines 1821-1844: Funding source dropdown for completed expenses with data-testid='funding-source-completed-{expense.id}'
Frontend AchatsTab.jsx line 1662 & 1822: Both dropdowns correctly gated by currentUser?.role === 'admin'
seed_data_creation: Created test expenses (50000 F) and test accounts (10000 F) for testing insufficient balance scenarios. All test data cleaned up after tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 61 added smart expense allocation feature. Key points: (1) New endpoint POST /api/expenses/{id}/allocate-account-smart with 3 modes: topup_existing (auto top-up account), create_new (create dedicated account), allow_negative (allow overdraft). (2) Frontend dropdown '💰 Payé depuis :' now visible on BOTH 'Achats validés' AND 'Achats terminés' views for Admin only. (3) When selecting account with insufficient balance, window.prompt appears with 3 options. (4) Top-up label format: 'Recharge auto pour <description>'. (5) Account top_ups[] array tracks all auto top-ups. (6) Old endpoint /api/expenses/{id}/allocate-account still works for sufficient balance cases (regression verified).

backend: POST /api/expenses/{id}/allocate-account-smart with mode='topup_existing' - VERIFIED (account topped up from 10000 to 50000 F)
POST /api/expenses/{id}/allocate-account-smart with mode='create_new' - VERIFIED (new account created with expense amount)
POST /api/expenses/{id}/allocate-account-smart with mode='allow_negative' - VERIFIED (balance goes negative)
Top-up label format 'Recharge auto pour <description>' - VERIFIED
Account top_ups[] array updated with top-up entry - VERIFIED
Validation: Invalid mode rejected with 400 - VERIFIED
Validation: Missing account_id for topup_existing rejected - VERIFIED
Completed expenses can be allocated - VERIFIED
frontend: Dropdown '💰 Payé depuis :' visible on 'Achats validés' for Admin - VERIFIED
Dropdown '💰 Payé depuis :' visible on 'Achats terminés' for Admin - VERIFIED (data-testid='funding-source-completed-{id}')
Dropdown NOT visible for Manager/Gérante - VERIFIED (0 dropdowns found)
window.prompt triggered when selecting account with insufficient balance - VERIFIED
Prompt message contains account name, balance, missing amount, and 3 options - VERIFIED
Toast notification 'Compte rechargé de X F et dépense imputée.' - VERIFIED
Account balance updated in dropdown after allocation - VERIFIED

Frontend: Verify dropdown visible for approved expense - SUCCESS
Frontend: Select account with insufficient balance - SUCCESS
Frontend: Verify window.prompt triggered with correct message - SUCCESS
